Migration guide for upgrading to MBC CQR: Migration guide for upgrading to MBC CQRS Serverless v1.2.0, including publishSync null return behavior.
This guide helps you upgrade from v1.1.x: This guide helps you upgrade from v1.1.x to v1.2.0. Version 1.2.0 includes one breaking change that requires code updates.
Add null check before using the result: Add null check before using the result
Search for `publishSync()` and `publishP: Search for `publishSync()` and `publishPartialUpdateSync()` calls and add null checks
Run the full test suite after upgrading: Run the full test suite after upgrading
Breaking Change 1: publishSync Returns n: Breaking Change 1: publishSync Returns null on No-op
now return `null` when the command is no: now return `null` when the command is not dirty (the payload is identical to the existing version). This aligns with the behavior of `publishAsync()` and `publishPartialUpdateAsync()`.
For each call site, wrap the result in a: For each call site, wrap the result in a null check before accessing any properties:
`publishSync` was previously inconsisten: `publishSync` was previously inconsistent with `publishAsync` — the async variants already returned `null` on no-op. This change makes all four publish methods consistent and avoids confusing behavior where `publishSync` returned the existing record even when no write occurred.
Add null checks for `publishSync` and `p: Add null checks for `publishSync` and `publishPartialUpdateSync` results (see above)
**Read-Your-Writes (RYW) consistency** —: **Read-Your-Writes (RYW) consistency** — after `publishAsync`, subsequent reads by the same user return pending command data before DynamoDB Stream sync completes. Opt-in via `RYW_SESSION_TTL_MINUTES` environment variable. See [CommandService: Read-Your-Writes](/docs/command-service#read-your-writes) for setup.
